title: Grifting 101

jokes.text: Well, the candy has lost its appeal now that I work at the store. You might say becoming a teacher here gave me diabetes.
jokes.setup: Jeff compares himself to a kid in a candy store with the course catalog
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor that working at Greendale literally gave him diabetes
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor that perfectly captures Jeff's jadedness about Greendale. The diabetes twist is unexpected and character-specific.

jokes.text: Are you always thirsty and fatigued and you have blurry vision and your cuts heal slower? Those are actual diabetes symptoms. That's what the doctor said.
jokes.setup: Jeff's metaphorical diabetes comment
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Jeff actually has real diabetes symptoms and saw a doctor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that transforms a metaphor into uncomfortable reality. The timing and Annie's medical knowledge make it land perfectly.

jokes.text: He said, make an appointment through his office during business hours. This is his home, his children sleep here.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Jeff seeing a doctor for treatment
jokes.punchline: Jeff went to the doctor's house instead of the office
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jeff's self-centeredness and poor boundaries. Delivered with casual matter-of-factness.

jokes.text: I'll tell you for five bucks.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what grifters do
jokes.punchline: Britta immediately tries to monetize basic information
jokes.explanation: Quick, economical joke that shows Britta's misguided entrepreneurial spirit. Simple but effective character beat.

jokes.text: I've always wanted to learn more about grifting ever since I saw 1990's The Grifters, featuring John Cusack, Anjelica Huston, and almost no grifting.
jokes.setup: Abed expressing interest in learning about grifting
jokes.punchline: The movie called 'The Grifters' contains almost no actual grifting
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed meta-commentary on film tropes. The specificity and casual film criticism is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Even more so than by that oceanography class, only none of you will even get a goldfish
jokes.setup: Warning that they'll get grifted by the grifting class
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous disappointing class that at least gave them goldfish
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that establishes Greendale's pattern of disappointing classes. The goldfish detail adds specificity.

jokes.text: Oh, this is one of those things where Jeff gets jealous of something dumb.
jokes.setup: Jeff's resistance to the grifting class
jokes.punchline: Annie recognizes and names Jeff's predictable character pattern
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-awareness of sitcom character patterns. Annie breaking the fourth wall by recognizing Jeff's recurring behavior.

jokes.text: That's something even I could mock, and I live in an RV. I'm judging you, and my dinner table folds down from a wall.
jokes.setup: Jeff being jealous of a grifter
jokes.punchline: Even Chang, who lives in an RV with a fold-down table, can judge Jeff
jokes.explanation: Great escalating structure with specific visual details. Chang's self-awareness of his low status makes the judgment funnier.

jokes.text: I'll pay you back. I'll get it from your parents. I'll pay you back. At which point I'll pay back your parents. Which will be never. I'll get a high five from your parents.
jokes.setup: Britta asking someone to cover her for the grifting class
jokes.punchline: Increasingly convoluted and nonsensical repayment plan ending with high fives
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity that perfectly captures Britta's financial irresponsibility and twisted logic.

jokes.text: Basic my ass, that's advanced.
jokes.setup: Learning they need briefcases for 'Basic' grifting
jokes.punchline: Annie points out the contradiction in needing equipment for a 'basic' class
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation. Annie's earnest confusion with mild profanity is endearing.

jokes.text: Shocker. Are they regulation?
jokes.setup: Learning DeSalvo has extra briefcases available
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sarcastic 'shocker' about the obvious setup
jokes.explanation: Jeff's cynicism proving correct. Simple sarcasm that moves the plot forward while staying in character.

jokes.text: It's a grift. What's a regulation briefcase? I don't know. But they're provided for only $150.
jokes.setup: Students asking about regulation briefcases
jokes.punchline: DeSalvo admits he doesn't know what they are but sells them anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of the grift in action. The casual admission of ignorance while still charging money is beautifully brazen.

jokes.text: Guys, watch this, can I borrow $150? No. See!
jokes.setup: Jeff asking to borrow money after criticizing the grift
jokes.punchline: Immediate rejection proves his point about being taken advantage of
jokes.explanation: Clever demonstration of his point through action rather than argument. The immediate 'No' and Jeff's vindicated 'See!' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: I'll pay you back. I'll get it from your parents. Tell them I spit on their wealth and thank you.
jokes.setup: Britta repeating her repayment scheme
jokes.punchline: Adding an insult to the parents she expects to pay
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds the perfect Britta touch of anti-establishment rhetoric mixed with dependence on wealth.

jokes.text: My name is Professor Roger DeSalvo, for the time being. For I use many a pseudonym, such as Jeff Goulash, Philip Switch, or Baz Ravish.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo introducing himself as a grifter professor
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake names that sound like bad spy aliases
jokes.explanation: The fake names are delightfully absurd and establish DeSalvo's theatrical, over-the-top character. 'Jeff Goulash' is particularly silly.

jokes.text: For I use many a pseudonym, such as Jeff Goulash, Philip Switch, or Baz Ravish.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo lists his fake names
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous fake names, especially 'Jeff Goulash'
jokes.explanation: Fun wordplay that establishes character while being quotably silly. 'Jeff Goulash' is particularly memorable nonsense.

jokes.text: Now which one of you thinks I did that on purpose? Exactly, you think you've seen me fail. You think you've spotted a weakness.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo apparently failing at something (dropping his hat)
jokes.punchline: Claiming the failure was intentional as a grifting demonstration
jokes.explanation: Classic con-artist move of turning incompetence into supposed strategy. Establishes DeSalvo's method of always claiming to be in control.

jokes.text: Pass them like hell. Pass them like your sister's dying, and continue that for about 40 minutes.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo having them pass briefcases quickly
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic urgency followed by the mundane '40 minutes'
jokes.explanation: The escalation from 'hell' to 'dying sister' to the prosaic '40 minutes' is well-structured. Shows the absurdity of the exercise.

jokes.text: That sounds like a real name, not the name of a grifter and therefore, not a teacher. My name is Slick Pockets Tucker. No, it's not.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo criticizing Annie's real name
jokes.punchline: Annie immediately adopts and then abandons a ridiculous fake name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie moment - eager to please but immediately honest. 'Slick Pockets Tucker' is a great fake grifter name, and her instant honesty is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: briefcase swapping talcum for grifters palm, briefcase hinge oil for silence switcheroos, and something called extra believable socks. Those are actually half off, I kind of feel like I grifted him.
jokes.setup: Listing the ridiculous products DeSalvo tried to sell
jokes.punchline: Student thinking they got a deal on 'extra believable socks'
jokes.explanation: The product names are brilliantly absurd, especially 'extra believable socks.' The student's pride at getting a 'deal' on fake products is perfect.

jokes.text: Jeff, you really need us to say it? You were... Eh, whoa, whoa, yeah I need you to say it. Let me just get settled. Okay go. You were right.
jokes.setup: The group about to admit Jeff was right
jokes.punchline: Jeff making them wait while he gets comfortable to hear it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff moment of narcissistic satisfaction. The physical comedy of him 'getting settled' to savor the moment is beautifully in character.

jokes.text: My feelings are fine. I'm just very lazy, and you gave me an excuse to do nothing. I'm not just gonna throw that away.
jokes.setup: Students apologizing for hurting Jeff's feelings
jokes.punchline: Jeff admitting he's just lazy and likes having an excuse to do nothing
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest Jeff moment that perfectly captures his character. The casual admission of laziness as motivation is refreshingly direct.

jokes.text: Shouldn't you be on some kind of reverse pissing contest to determine who's laziest? Wait, is that where you suck it back up in your? I'm sorry, I've only ever done it the other way. You mean peeing? No, I mean in the back.
jokes.setup: Britta trying to motivate Jeff by comparing him to DeSalvo in laziness
jokes.punchline: Britta getting confused about the mechanics of 'reverse pissing'
jokes.explanation: Classic Britta moment of taking a metaphor too literally and making it awkward. The confusion about bodily functions is peak Britta.

jokes.text: Well, I was the guy with a full bottle of scotch and a high tolerance for foreigners.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo asking Jeff to identify himself
jokes.punchline: Jeff's casual xenophobia disguised as tolerance
jokes.explanation: Dark character moment showing Jeff's casual prejudices. The 'tolerance' phrasing makes it both funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: If you need a mnemonic device, it's the one without someone else's stuff all over it.
jokes.setup: Jeff showing DeSalvo to his desk
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate way to say 'the empty desk'
jokes.explanation: Jeff's unnecessarily verbose way of pointing out the obvious. The 'mnemonic device' framing is nicely pretentious.

jokes.text: Like a string is a sweater? Like, paint is the Sistine Chapel? Sure, grifting includes lying, but only a liar would call grifting lying.
jokes.setup: Argument about whether grifting is just lying
jokes.punchline: DeSalvo's increasingly grandiose analogies ending in circular logic
jokes.explanation: Beautiful escalation from simple craft analogy to high art, then the circular logic of the final line. Perfectly captures DeSalvo's pretentious character.

jokes.text: And, only a delusional hobo would glorify stealing. And, I'm God's paintbrush. No, I am God, which pretty much makes me better than you.
jokes.setup: The argument escalating between Jeff and DeSalvo
jokes.punchline: Increasingly grandiose self-descriptions culminating in claiming to be God
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure where both characters try to one-up each other into absurdity. The progression to claiming godhood is beautifully ridiculous.

jokes.text: I wanna speak to you, you son of a bitch. What? I love you. Oh. I do too. Good.
jokes.setup: Jeff angrily confronting DeSalvo
jokes.punchline: Sudden declaration of love instead of conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection from anger to affection. The sudden emotional pivot is absurd and the mutual 'I love you' exchange is beautifully awkward.

jokes.text: Or, the friction has gradually melted the handle to fit into our palms.
jokes.setup: Asked if they're getting better at briefcase passing
jokes.punchline: Grimly literal explanation of why the exercise feels easier
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny observation about the pointless exercise. The image of handles melting into palms is both absurd and grimly realistic.

jokes.text: The next person who says grift is gonna grift! My god, now it's replacing other words.
jokes.setup: Annie getting frustrated with the word 'grift'
jokes.punchline: The word has infected her speech, replacing normal words
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about how repeated words lose meaning and start replacing other words. Annie's horror at the linguistic infection is perfect.

jokes.text: Telegram for Elroy Patashnik. Oh, my God. My Aunt Baba died on vacation while in Africa. But she left me a million dollars, but the African government wants to seize the funds.
jokes.setup: Elroy receiving a telegram
jokes.punchline: Classic Nigerian prince email scam delivered as a telegram
jokes.explanation: Brilliant updating of the Nigerian prince scam to a vintage telegram format. The 'Aunt Baba' detail and formal delivery make it funnier.

jokes.text: The African telegram. The Jim Belushi of grift? Are you trying to grift me?
jokes.setup: DeSalvo recognizing the telegram scam
jokes.punchline: Calling it the 'Jim Belushi of grift' - ubiquitous but not respected
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture analogy comparing the overused scam to Jim Belushi - everywhere but not particularly good. Perfect encapsulation of the scam's reputation.

jokes.text: The Brown Betty? The Texas oil baby? The reverse Jim Gaffigan?
jokes.setup: Jeff pretending to know advanced grifting techniques
jokes.punchline: Made-up grift names including one based on a real comedian
jokes.explanation: Creative fake grift names with good variety. The 'reverse Jim Gaffigan' is particularly funny as an inexplicable comedian reference.

jokes.text: Sir, you. That guy left his lottery ticket on the table. That was the telegram man. No, it wasn't. I mean what telegram?
jokes.setup: Chang pointing out a lottery ticket
jokes.punchline: DeSalvo pretending there was no telegram man while poorly executing the coverup
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection attempt that falls apart immediately. DeSalvo's bad cover-up reveals his amateur status as a grifter.

jokes.text: I'm gonna grift you. I'm gonna grift your ass. Or is that the grift?
jokes.setup: DeSalvo getting increasingly agitated
jokes.punchline: Escalating threats followed by paranoid self-doubt
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing DeSalvo losing composure, then the paranoid turn where he questions his own reality. Character work pays off.

jokes.text: Can an idiot walk backwards like this and not hit anything? Maybe. Or, maybe not.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo trying to prove he's not an idiot
jokes.punchline: Presumably walks into something while walking backwards
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup. The timing and DeSalvo's confidence making the pratfall funnier when it inevitably happens.

jokes.text: I didn't know you and Winger were lovers. If he wanted my respect, he should have opened with that.
jokes.setup: Britta mentioning Jeff's obsession with DeSalvo
jokes.punchline: DeSalvo assuming they're romantic partners and being impressed by it
jokes.explanation: Funny misinterpretation that reveals DeSalvo's values - he'd respect Jeff more if they were lovers. Unexpected character detail.

jokes.text: Because, oh, my god, Jeff is making this up as we go.
jokes.setup: Annie realizing Jeff doesn't actually have a plan
jokes.punchline: Breaking the fourth wall about the improvised nature of the plot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Annie recognizes the show's own improvisational writing. Self-aware comedy that works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: I guess at any of the several dozen points when a normal person would say, by the way, I have no plan.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking when he could have planned a grift
jokes.punchline: Pointing out Jeff had many opportunities to admit he had no plan
jokes.explanation: Solid logic that exposes Jeff's poor communication. The 'several dozen points' exaggeration makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Okay, just so you know, if I do and we have half Sting children, when they ask me how to grift, I'm gonna tell them to watch their mother, not listen to you!
jokes.setup: Jeff sarcastically suggesting Abed marry The Sting movie
jokes.punchline: Abed taking it literally and planning their theoretical children's grifting education
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed moment taking Jeff's sarcasm literally and building an elaborate scenario. The idea of half-Sting children is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: I need to be better than this guy. So let me think.
jokes.setup: Jeff's emotional breakdown about needing to win
jokes.punchline: His simple, honest admission of petty motivation
jokes.explanation: Vulnerable Jeff moment that's both pathetic and relatable. The simplicity of his need to win is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: That movie was terrible. You slept the whole time. Yeah, that's worse. The dialogue seeped into my dreams. My whole brain is long and quiet.
jokes.setup: Students' reaction to watching The Sting
jokes.punchline: Student's poetic description of how bad dialogue infected their dreams
jokes.explanation: Beautiful, poetic description of boring movie dialogue. 'My whole brain is long and quiet' is wonderfully evocative and funny.

jokes.text: Holed in the closet watching The Sting like gypsies.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo finding them hiding while watching the movie
jokes.punchline: Comparing them to gypsies for their furtive movie-watching
jokes.explanation: DeSalvo's theatrical way of describing their situation. The comparison works but the ethnic reference is dated.

jokes.text: No, I can't think of a metaphor pathetic enough to suit you, Jeff Winger, though I am reminded of something Oscar Wilde once said when he compared sheep to wolves.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo trying to insult Jeff with a metaphor
jokes.punchline: Can't think of one, then makes up a fake Oscar Wilde quote
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing DeSalvo's pretentiousness and inability to follow through. The fake Oscar Wilde quote is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: You hit me with a woman's hand. You Midwestern floozy. I lived in New York.
jokes.setup: Fight between Jeff and DeSalvo
jokes.punchline: Insults about fighting style and geography
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate insults. DeSalvo's theatrical language and Jeff's defensive geography correction work for both characters.

jokes.text: Grift blood. Someone taste it. I'm sure it's Karo Syrup and food dye. I mean I would, but my diabetes.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo appears to be bleeding
jokes.punchline: Jeff's diabetes prevents him from tasting fake blood
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the diabetes plot thread. The dark humor of Jeff being unable to help due to his medical condition is perfect.

jokes.text: So sorry guys. This is all my fault. Don't be. I'm the one who got in a pissing match. With a jerk. You're a jerk. You're all jerks. And this whole school can suck a big one.
jokes.setup: Jeff apologizing for the situation
jokes.punchline: DeSalvo's increasingly broad insults ending with the whole school
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing DeSalvo's petulant nature. The progression from personal to institutional insults works well.

jokes.text: Speaking on that topic, Britta, this is all your fault and you're expelled.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to handle the situation
jokes.punchline: Frankie randomly blaming and expelling Britta
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frankie moment of cold efficiency. The randomness of blaming Britta and immediate expulsion is brilliantly arbitrary.

jokes.text: She started it as a, as a, as a, as a, as a, as a fake biology group so that she could have sex with Troy.
jokes.setup: Jeff defends Britta as the heart of the group
jokes.punchline: His stammering revelation of her selfish original motivation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the series origin with Jeff's characteristic stammering when he realizes his argument backfired. The repetition shows his panic.

jokes.text: She started it as a fake biology group so that she could have sex with Troy. No. That's exactly it. Oh, okay. Don't let me handle it.
jokes.setup: Jeff defending Britta's importance to the group
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals the study group's sexual origins, Dean agrees it's bad
jokes.explanation: Good callback to series origins that backfires on Jeff. The Dean's immediate agreement and Jeff's retreat is well-timed.

jokes.text: You can't expel her with four week-isodes left in the seaso-mester.
jokes.setup: Jeff desperately defending Britta
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall with TV terminology mixed with school terms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke about the show's episode structure. 'Week-isodes' and 'seaso-mester' are perfect Community-style neologisms.

jokes.text: Now that you're expelled, you might consider a career in grifting. You're quite good at it.
jokes.setup: Britta being expelled after helping with the grift
jokes.punchline: DeSalvo complimenting her grifting skills as career advice
jokes.explanation: Nice irony that Britta, who got expelled for the grift, is the one with actual talent. DeSalvo's professional compliment adds insult to injury.

jokes.text: Some say I'm a lucky grifter. Some say I'm the king. They call me the lucky grifter. They call me the King of England. I am the king.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo singing about his reputation
jokes.punchline: Escalating from lucky grifter to King of England
jokes.explanation: Classic DeSalvo grandiosity escalating to absurd levels. The repetitive structure mirrors folk songs while building to ridiculous claims.

jokes.text: Briefcase parade! Who the hell are you? You get back here. You get back here with my money.
jokes.setup: Mass briefcase confusion as the grift concludes
jokes.punchline: DeSalvo's desperation as he chases the briefcase parade
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to all the briefcase passing. The 'briefcase parade' and DeSalvo's panic creates satisfying chaos and resolution.

jokes.text: Hey, whoa pal. That usage may fly in the UK, not in the States. I have a mentally challenged cousin. Sister. Pics. I wanna see pics.
jokes.setup: Chang correcting DeSalvo's use of 'mental'
jokes.punchline: Chang's correction escalating to inappropriate requests for photos
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang moment where appropriate political correctness quickly becomes inappropriate voyeurism. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Because it sounds like he was either the victim of crass, artless thuggery. Correct. Or Professor DeSalvo was targeted by a group of people he vastly underestimated.
jokes.setup: Jeff laying out two possibilities for what happened
jokes.punchline: DeSalvo immediately agreeing to the insulting option
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup where Jeff offers two interpretations and DeSalvo immediately chooses the one that insults him. Perfect trap construction.

jokes.text: I was grifted. Grift, grift, grift, grift, grift, grift, grift, grift.
jokes.setup: DeSalvo finally admitting he was grifted
jokes.punchline: Everyone repeating 'grift' in celebration
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback to the word infection problem from earlier. The chanted repetition makes it a celebration of victory and linguistic absurdity.

jokes.text: My name is Ryan Ridley. Three years ago my life was saved by a stranger at my gym. I felt like I owed him my life. Apparently he agreed. Now he has me do things, and I keep hoping and praying that one day I'll do enough for him to call us even. Until then, I'm just a guy from his gym.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining his relationship with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Revealing Jeff has been exploiting his life debt for years
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy revelation about Jeff's character. The earnest delivery makes the exploitation funnier and more disturbing. Perfect Community-style moral ambiguity.

tmdb_overview: Visiting Professor DeSalvo gives Jeff and his friends a master class in grifting and a bonus lesson on the art of revenge.
insight: Con-artist chaos sustains 84 laughs through relentless escalation and character absurdity.
